expected.summary: The council discussed awarding a contract to HDR Engineering for engineering and architectural design services for the Schumaker Bridge Replacement Project in an amount not to exceed 4,000,700. Speakers expressed strong support for the project, noted its importance for safety, beautification, connectivity, and park integration, and described future funding and coordination needs.
action_items: Award the contract to HDR Engineering for the Schumaker Bridge Replacement Project
Move the contract forward to begin design work
Coordinate with partners and stakeholders on the project
Seek additional funding for the remaining design and construction needs
risks: Only about 30% of the funding needed for the design portion is currently covered
The project will require significant state and local funding
Construction is pending future funding
open_questions: Will the initial study be used with early action funds?

expected.summary: Speaker B explains that speaker and meeting statistics are already processed offline into objects and discusses how that data should be returned to the interface for display. The conversation also covers GUI priorities, loading and searching meetings, start-up screen ideas, meeting and speaker selection, search ranking with TF-IDF, and how to structure results and future meetings.
action_items: Return speaker and meeting data in a format that is easy for the GUI to display
Leave methods that return one value at a time for the interface
Finish the object processing before sharing the final version globally
Design the GUI and startup screen before finalizing the rest of the interface
Consider using keywords or TF-IDF for ranking search results
Decide how search results should be ranked and how many to show
Meet again after S_P_2 or on Friday around three o'clock if possible
risks: Changing the classes after sharing them would invalidate the stored object files
Loading many engines or meetings the wrong way can crash the system
Without ranking, highly relevant search results may fall too low in the results list
open_questions: What data format is easiest for the GUI to display?
Should the startup screen default to search or quick meeting access?
How should meetings and speakers be represented in the startup interface?
Should search results be ranked with TF-IDF or left unranked?
How should multiple search terms be combined and ranked?

input_text:
Project: TFSAP
Issue Type: Bug
Status: Open
Summary: Reject artifact property changes if no repository is specified
Description: The ADS task [ArtifactoryProperties@1 |https://www.jfrog.com/confluence/display/JFROG/Artifactory+Azure+DevOps+Extension#ArtifactoryAzureDevOpsExtension-SettingandDeletingPropertiesonFilesinArtifactory] can be used without specifying a repository in the `pattern` argument. This means that you can apply your changes to all artifacts that match the given wildcard expression everywhere in Artifactory. As an example, a developer accidentally called the task like this: {code:java} - task: ArtifactoryProperties@1 inputs: command: 'set' artifactoryService: 'Artifactory' setProps: 'someproperty=1;' specSource: 'taskConfiguration' fileSpec: | { "files": [ { "pattern": "*.zip" } ] } {code} This resulted in all our .zip files everywhere in artifactory to get this property. Even .zip files that were part of other packages. It is the same functionality if you use the `delete` function instead of `set`. We haven't tested it for obvious reasons, but it appears that using the pattern: {code:java} "pattern": "*.*" {code} Would apply the changes to all artifacts everywhere in artifactory. This means a developer can accidentally, or maliciously, overwrite or delete critical artifact properties, like buildNumber and buildName, for all artifacts that have been uploaded. Even cached artifacts will be changed. For companies who have to work under regulatory conditions this is extremely critical. Naturally it can be reverted from a backup, but that means that you have to be aware that the changes have happened to begin with. I think it is critical that the task is changed so that it should require a repository as a minimum. Ideally it should go even further, so that it is only allowed to add properties and not delete/overwrite existing properties without a user with Admin privileges. Just because a "user" has deploy permissions should not enable them to overwrite/delete properties, only add them.
Comments:
1. [~Dakimaz] Thanks for your feedback. I'll pass it on to our product team. To limit the search results you can set the repository name as part of the pattern. You can also filter, for example, by properties or by build: {code:java} { "files": [ { "pattern": "repoName/*.zip", "props": "keyToFilter=valueToFilter", "build": "buildNameToFilter/buildNumberToFilter" } ] } {code} The File Spec is a powerf

expected.summary: This open bug report says the Azure DevOps ArtifactoryProperties task can change artifact properties across all repositories if no repository is specified in the pattern. The reporter says this can accidentally or maliciously overwrite or delete critical properties for many artifacts and argues that a repository should be required at minimum.
action_items: Require a repository to be specified when changing artifact properties
Restrict property deletion or overwrite actions unless performed by a user with Admin privileges
risks: A developer can accidentally or maliciously overwrite or delete critical properties for artifacts across all repositories
Cached artifacts can also be modified
The issue is especially critical for companies operating under regulatory conditions

Project: STORM
Issue Type: Story
Status: Open
Summary: Inworld Posing Feature
Description: h2. The Problem Ever since the very first shape was created there was one big issue with animations. Animations are oddly specific to the avatar size and more importantly individual bone size, which causes a lot of problems when animating or simply just posing. You sit on a chair that was made for the default avatar size but you are smaller than that? Tough luck, your legs will most likely clip into the chair, your arms will hover above the leans and you will most likely not even properly sit in that chair either. With the introduction of hover height we got a simple quick way of fixing at least the height so we don't sink into the ground or whatever object we are sitting on, however, the main issue remains, most poses will hardly fit anyone whose shape doesn't match that of the rigging template that was used and even in those cases often times your hands clip into your attachments or worse. h2. The Solution Inworld, real-time posing. The ability to rotate all available bones (133 currently) including those of Bento at any time, anywhere we want as long as they follow the bone hierarchy. This feature allows us to make small scale changes to existing poses (and animations) without having the original creator to make a special version for you only, it also allows us to add great detail to poses that weren't previously fitted with these. One example of this would be adding Bento bone overrides to a pose that was created without Bento in mind, no more stiff hands, no more tails or wings clipping into walls and furniture, you get full control over every single bone of your avatar, given it is rigged to one. This would allow us not only to fix every pose for our specific needs but create completely new ones on the fly, made directly inworld for our avatar to fit perfectly. !7.PNG! h2. Bonus The current Poser comes with additional features that allows it to enable and disable every bone override separately, this allows you to specify a bone override to be used for a single bone or a small group of bones, allowing the underneath pose or animation to continue playing without interruptions. An example for this would be holding a stick in your hand while walking. You'd enable the bone overrides for the shoulder, upper and lower arm and the hand as well as all fingers, create a holding pose and save it or keep it running. You'll end up holding the stick just like you want while all other animations such as walking, crouching, running etc will continue to work normally. !8.PNG! Ontop of bone overrides the current Poser also has a feature similar to Gesture queues simply called the "Animator" currently, it allows saved poses to be put into a list, together with WAIT and RESTART commands in order to play them in order and thus create a basic animation, this can be used in many ways and can be scaled in complexity up to the point that you are playing hundreds of saved poses in a row all with different bone overrides creating a smooth, perfectly fitted animation for a scene or a theater for example. The possibilities are endless. h2. Server This is the primary missing feature currently. There is absolutely no synchronization between Viewers, this would need either Viewer specific synchronization, which would lock out all Viewers not having this feature or full server side support allowing to send poses or pose changes to other clients the same way Hover Height currently works. h2. Export Currently the Poser has no means of exporting saved poses other than the locally saved XML files it creates when saving a pose. I was planning to include BVH or ANIM export support but for that to work i'd first need some extensive knowledge how BVH/ANIM files look and work. h2. Abuse I heard the complains about possibly stealing poses or entire animations. There are many easy ways to fix these should export be a thing, many of which simply include blocking the export function unless you specifically chose to start your posing from an empty T Pose. Even without it i hardly see an issue here. The poser is meant to be the ultimate posing tool, making poses as we know them today mostly obsolete, sold poses are already given away for free since they are practically worthless, with a Poser that allows you to pose any bone at any time anyway you want they would simply become even less than that, they would become a simple inventory object whose only purposes are saving your pose (permanently) on the server or sharing it to others. There is no stealing anyone's poses if anyone can easily make any possibly pose directly in world in a few minutes and fit it for their avatar on top of that. Clinging to worthless 10L$ poses will only ever limit and ultimately break this feature, making it less and less attractive until there is simply no point having it in the first place. ---- I'd simply recommend trying the feature out yourself before making any assumptions. I

expected.summary: This open story proposes an inworld real-time posing feature that would let users rotate all available avatar bones to adjust or create poses directly inworld. The description also highlights bone overrides, an animator system, and the need for synchronization support so poses can be shared reliably across viewers.
action_items: Add inworld real-time posing for all available avatar bones
Support selective bone overrides that can layer over existing animations
Add synchronization of poses or pose changes across viewers
Consider adding pose export support such as BVH or ANIM
risks: Without synchronization support, poses will not be shared consistently across viewers
Viewer-specific synchronization could lock out viewers that do not support the feature
Export functionality could raise concerns about copying or sharing poses and animations
